title: T-139 — PARITY: FA detail shell Playwright + axe parity (vs fa-screens.jsx:300-452) NPD-a slice of T-020
prompt:
# T-139 — FA detail shell parity check

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md §3, §5 + `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/fa-screens.jsx:300-452 (fa_detail + fa_right_panel)
Parent root: T-020

## Goal
Playwright screenshot parity + axe-violations check for the assembled FA detail shell (tabs container + right panel) against the prototype. Validates layout, tab switching, right panel visibility across tabs, and 0 critical/serious axe violations.

## Implementation contract
1. apps/web/e2e/npd/fa-detail-shell-parity.spec.ts: seeds an FA row + gateProgress fixture; visits /(npd)/fa/PRJ-1?tab=core — captures full-page screenshot — switches ?tab=technical — captures — switches ?tab=history — captures.
2. Each screenshot compared against the corresponding prototype region with the prototype-parity tolerance configured in repo Playwright config.
3. axe-core run on each tab after mount; assert 0 critical and 0 serious violations.
4. Tab switching tested in-spec: switching does not full-reload; right panel remains visible on every tab.

## Files
- apps/web/e2e/npd/fa-detail-shell-parity.spec.ts [create]
- apps/web/e2e/npd/fixtures/fa-detail-seed.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the FA detail route renders, when Playwright captures full-page screenshots across multiple tabs, then each view matches the fa-screens.jsx:300-452 prototype region within the configured parity tolerance per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`
2. Given each tab is mounted, when axe-core runs, then 0 critical and 0 serious violations are reported per the parity policy
3. Given a user switches tabs (Core → Technical → History), when each tab activates, then the right panel remains visible (no remount, no flicker) — verified by Playwright DOM-stability assertion

## Test strategy
- RED: fa-detail-shell-parity.spec.ts before T-138 wiring closes; ensures wiring keeps the shell + right panel in parity
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test:e2e fa-detail-shell-parity'

## Risk red lines
- Do not run against shared/staging DB — use isolated test container
- Do not relax tolerance to make tests pass
- Do not skip axe checks on any tab
